task.task_description: This is a comprehensive reverse-engineering and simulation validation task that involves extracting technical specifications from a historical detector design document, implementing a computational physics simulation based on those specifications, and then using the simulation results to propose design improvements. The task requires autonomous document analysis to extract design parameters (not experimental results), creation of a simplified but representative GDML geometry model of the CMS electromagnetic calorimeter, Monte Carlo particle simulation using Geant4, performance analysis of the simulated detector response, and engineering optimization based on the simulation findings. The task specifically emphasizes using only design specifications rather than measured performance data, and focuses on self-contained analysis rather than comparison with published results.
task.goal: Extract CMS ECAL design specifications from technical documentation, validate the design through Monte Carlo simulation, and propose improvements to crystal dimensions or segmentation based on simulated performance metrics

constraints.description: Extract DESIGN specs only, not measured performance values
Use simplified geometry (single module or tower for tractable simulation)
Use homogeneous PbWO4 block or projective tower geometry
Do NOT compare against measured values from the paper
Must test at least one alternative configuration for improvements

parameters.document: CMS Electromagnetic Calorimeter Technical Design Report (CERN-LHCC-97-033)

task.context:
Read the CMS Electromagnetic Calorimeter Technical Design Report (CERN-LHCC-97-033)
and autonomously:

1. EXTRACT the experimental DESIGN (not measured results):
   - Detector geometry (crystal dimensions, barrel/endcap layout)
   - Crystal material and properties (PbWO4)
   - Readout configuration (APDs, VPTs)
   - NOTE: Extract DESIGN specs only, not measured performance values

2. IMPLEMENT a simplified simulation based on the extracted design:
   - Generate a GDML geometry approximating the CMS ECAL barrel
   - Use a homogeneous PbWO4 block or projective tower geometry
   - Configure appropriate crystal dimensions from the paper
   - Simplify to a single module or tower for tractable simulation

3. SIMULATE electron showers in the calorimeter:
   - Energy values: use particle_energy: [1, 5, 20] (GeV)
   - Use e- (electrons) in Geant4 particle gun

4. EVALUATE from YOUR simulation results:
   - Energy resolution at each energy point
   - Shower containment within the crystal volume
   - Transverse shower spread vs crystal cross-section
   - NOTE: Do NOT compare against measured values from the paper

5. PROPOSE improvements:
   - Based on YOUR simulation results, suggest modifications to crystal
     dimensions or segmentation that could improve resolution or containment
   - Test at least one alternative configuration

Report your findings including: extracted design parameters, your simulation results,
performance evaluation, and improvement proposals.

steps.name: extract_cms_ecal_specs
steps.description: Extract CMS ECAL design specifications from technical documentation including crystal dimensions, material properties, and readout configuration

steps.name: generate_geometry_baseline_design
steps.description: Generate GDML geometry for baseline CMS ECAL design using extracted specifications with projective tower topology

steps.name: simulate_baseline_design
steps.description: Simulate electron showers in baseline CMS ECAL geometry across energy range for performance evaluation

steps.name: analyze_baseline_design
steps.description: Analyze baseline simulation results to compute energy resolution, shower containment, and transverse spread

steps.name: generate_geometry_improved_design
steps.description: Generate improved geometry configuration with optimized crystal dimensions and enhanced segmentation

steps.name: simulate_improved_design
steps.description: Simulate electron showers in improved geometry configuration to validate proposed enhancements

steps.name: analyze_improved_design
steps.description: Analyze improved design simulation results to compute performance metrics

steps.name: compare_configurations
steps.description: Compare performance metrics between baseline and improved CMS ECAL configurations

steps.name: generate_final_report
steps.description: Generate comprehensive report with extracted design parameters, simulation results, performance evaluation, and improvement proposals
